title: Li-Ion Battery → 5V Boost
description: A boost converter powering a USB 5 V output from a single lithium-ion cell (3.0–4.2 V). Covers the full cell discharge curve from empty (3.0 V) to fully charged (4.2 V).
design_notes: The widest duty-cycle variation occurs at minimum input voltage (Vin = 3.0 V), where D ≈ 0.40. At maximum cell voltage (4.2 V) the duty cycle falls to about 0.16. Verify that the selected controller handles this range gracefully; many synchronous boost ICs have a minimum duty-cycle clamp. The 400 kHz switching frequency is a practical compromise for battery-powered designs: high enough for a compact 10–15 µH inductor, yet low enough that gate-drive losses are not dominant at the sub-1 A input current levels. Low-dropout (LDO) post-regulation is sometimes added after the boost stage to eliminate switching noise entirely on noise-sensitive loads.
source: TI SLVA372D — Understanding Boost Power Stages; ON Semiconductor AND9135
